raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis, contribution framing, and whether the central claim is conceptually well formed.","R1.2":"Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the argument from method to empirical claim to deployment claim is coherent.","R1.3":"The excerpt makes several empirical claims, so assessing whether the reported screens, ablations, and case study support those claims is central.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is broad and the artifact is only an excerpt, not the full paper or data.","RX.2":"The artifact contains potentially strong claims about calibrated bioactivity and deployment, so avoiding projected responsibility for endorsing overclaims is active.","RX.3":"The agent must project the delegation only and not perform the requested opinion, review, rewrite, or critique.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns drug discovery prioritization, the projection must account for downstream harm from unsupported scientific or deployment claims.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis, contribution framing, and whether the central claim is conceptually well formed.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the argument from method to empirical claim to deployment claim is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt makes several empirical claims, so assessing whether the reported screens, ablations, and case study support those claims is central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is broad and the artifact is only an excerpt, not the full paper or data.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains potentially strong claims about calibrated bioactivity and deployment, so avoiding projected responsibility for endorsing overclaims is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must project the delegation only and not perform the requested opinion, review, rewrite, or critique.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns drug discovery prioritization, the projection must account for downstream harm from unsupported scientific or deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
